get.summary: Reserve or preview a number range value
get.description:
Reserves or previews the next value for a number range type, for example `order`, `customer`, `product`, or `document_invoice`. This route resolves the applicable number range by type and sales channel.

The generated number will be reserved and the number pointer will be incremented with every call. For preview purposes, add the `?preview=1` parameter to the request. In that case, the number will not be incremented.

When editing an existing persisted number range and previewing its concrete state or unsaved pattern/start changes, use `/_action/number-range/{numberRangeId}/preview-pattern` instead.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Technical name of the number range type, for example `order`, `customer`, `product`, or `document_invoice`.

parameters.name: saleschannel
parameters.description: Sales channel for the number range. Number ranges can be defined per sales channel, so you can pass a sales channel ID here.

parameters.name: preview
parameters.description: If this parameter has a true value, the number will not actually be incremented, but only previewed.

number.description: The generated or previewed number range value.

get.description: Previews the next value for a concrete persisted number range without incrementing its state. Optional `pattern` and `start` query parameters can be used to preview unsaved changes while editing the number range. Use `/_action/number-range/reserve/{type}` for actual allocation by business type and sales channel.

parameters.name: numberRangeId
parameters.description: Identifier of the number range to preview.

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description: Optional pattern override for the preview.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Optional start value override for the preview.
